items.headline: CLARITY Act placed on Senate Legislative Calendar (General Orders No. 423), formally eligible for full Senate floor vote
items.summary: On June 1, 2026, a new version of the Senate Banking bill was published and the CLARITY Act was placed on the Senate Legislative Calendar under General Orders (Calendar No. 423), making it formally eligible for full Senate floor consideration. The CLARITY Act would grant the CFTC exclusive jurisdiction over digital commodity spot markets while maintaining SEC jurisdiction over investment contract assets.

Warsh stated inflation remained 'well above its 2% goal' and announced a five-area monetary policy task force review. CME FedWatch data showed ~80% probability of one or more rate hikes by end-2026, with 9 of 18 Fed members projecting a 2026 hike. Bitcoin initially dipped on the statement before partially recovering, while the Nasdaq briefly turned negative before also recovering.

items.headline: Strategy STRC and Strive SATA digital credit products suffer leverage liquidation selloff on June 18
items.summary: On June 18, 2026 — characterized as 'the most difficult day in the history of Digital Credit' by Strive CEO Matt Cole — Strategy's STRC fell to $82.50 and Strive's SATA dropped to the low $90s before both partially rebounded. Cole attributed the selloff to forced selling by leveraged investors rather than deterioration in credit quality. STRC later recovered to around $89, still well below its $100 par. CoinDesk coverage noted that concerns about STRC 'dominated market sentiment' and linked the event to broader crypto weakness on June 18.

items.headline: Microsoft Discloses CryptoBandits USB-Worm Malware Targeting Crypto Wallets via Clipboard Hijacking
items.summary: On June 19, Microsoft publicly disclosed Trojan:Win32/CryptoBandits, a sophisticated crypto-clipper worm active since at least February 2026. The malware spreads via infected USB drives using malicious .lnk shortcut files. Once installed, it monitors clipboard activity every 500 milliseconds, captures seed phrases, private keys, and wallet addresses for BTC and ETH wallets, exfiltrates data over the Tor network, and silently replaces copied wallet addresses with attacker-controlled ones before the victim pastes — redirecting transactions irreversibly. It also has remote code execution capability. Microsoft issued public guidance urging users to disable AutoRun, block .lnk execution from USB media, and verify wallet addresses before confirming transfers.

items.headline: Iran-Switzerland Peace Memorandum Signing Postponed Indefinitely After Israeli Lebanon Airstrikes
items.summary: Israel launched renewed airstrikes across southern Lebanon overnight, prompting Iran to refuse deployment of its delegation to the Bürgenstock resort in Switzerland. The formal US-Iran memorandum signing, scheduled for June 19, was postponed indefinitely. This removed the primary macro tailwind the crypto market had been pricing in to offset the Fed's hawkish June dot plot, triggering a broad crypto selloff: BTC -3%, ETH -3.26%, SOL -4.89%, XRP -4.61%, BNB -3.22%.

items.headline: Fed June FOMC Hawkish Dot Plot Residual Pressure: 9 Officials Project 2026 Rate Hike
items.summary: The June 17 Federal Reserve meeting kept rates unchanged but delivered a hawkish dot plot, with nine officials projecting a 2026 rate hike, eliminating market expectations for near-term rate cuts. The crypto market remained under pressure, with the total market cap sliding toward $2.1 trillion and the Fear & Greed Index remaining deep in Extreme Fear.

items.headline: Strategy's STRC Preferred Stock Hits Record Low; Strive CEO Blames Leverage Liquidations
items.summary: Strategy's STRC preferred stock dropped to a record low on or around June 19, closing well under its $100 par value after a monthly slide exceeding 10%. The decline renewed market concern that Strategy may need to sell Bitcoin holdings to fund dividend obligations. Strive CEO Matt Cole attributed the selloff in STRC and SATA to forced selling from leveraged investors — a cascade later described as a 'digital credit market selloff.' The STRC overhang dominated broader crypto market sentiment and contributed to BTC's fourth consecutive daily decline.

items.headline: Franklin Templeton Files SEC Registration for Two 'Bitcoin DRIP' ETFs That Reinvest Stock Dividends Into BTC
items.summary: Franklin Templeton filed with the SEC to launch two novel 'Bitcoin DRIP' ETFs that would hold US stocks and automatically reinvest cash dividends into Bitcoin rather than distributing them in cash, with an expected effective date as early as September 1, 2026. The filings represent a new ETF structural concept — embedding Bitcoin accumulation into traditional equity income products — and were prominently featured across crypto news feeds on June 19, 2026.

items.headline: CLARITY Act Remains on Senate Floor Calendar; White House Targets July 4 Signing — XRP Commodity Classification Key Outcome
items.summary: As of June 19, the CLARITY Act remained on the Senate floor calendar with a White House target signing date of July 4. The bill would permanently codify commodity classifications for major crypto assets including XRP, resolving the last major domestic legal overhang on the asset. Market participants noted this timeline was independent of the day's geopolitical disruption. The bill was described by multiple sources as the single most important domestic regulatory catalyst of the 2026 crypto cycle, with expected institutional flow implications across BTC, ETH, XRP, SOL, and BNB.

items.headline: Upbit Lists 9 Digital Assets Including LDO, PAXG, and MORPHO in Staggered Rollout
items.summary: South Korean exchange Upbit announced listing of nine digital assets across its BTC and USDT markets on June 19, including Lido DAO (LDO), PAX Gold (PAXG), Morpho (MORPHO), and six others. The listings were executed in staggered hourly windows between 3 PM and 7 PM Korea Standard Time. Upbit listings historically trigger significant short-term price movements in the listed assets due to the exchange's large South Korean retail base.

items.headline: Long-Term BTC Holders Absorb 125,000 BTC in June; Strategy Holds 846,842 BTC
